result:
Iteration complete. The experiment is pre-registered and implemented; the harness will now train, score, and decide keep/revert.

## What I designed and why

**Experiment 8 — Deployment-envelope residual encoder (232k → 973k params).** Category: architecture, from-scratch init.

The history pointed at one untested family. Experiments 3–5 (supervision/decode mechanisms) were all null; exp 6 (rotation resampling) was null; exp 7's 7.5× data scale-up — the fix all those failures jointly implied — recovered only 7% (2489 → 2326 m), meaning the model couldn't absorb the extra coverage. The decisive evidence came from the kept run's per-bucket breakdown: median errors are nearly **flat across lighting buckets** (Berlin night 1760 m vs. midday 1642 m). A lighting or supervision bottleneck would show a bucket gradient; a flat profile at ~2 km on a 7 km map is the signature of plain underfitting. Meanwhile the model used only 0.93 MB of the 4 MiB deployment gate and 1.25 ms of the 250 ms latency budget — encoder depth/width is the one axis no experiment had ever touched.

## The change (model/model.py only)

Replaced the 4-conv encoder with an 11-conv residual stack sized to the flight envelope: stem 3→32/s2, then three stride-2 stages (64, 128, 160 channels), each ending in a residual block — bottleneck blocks early where 32²/16² resolution makes compute expensive, a full 3×3 pair at 8² where parameters are cheap — then GAP to a 160-d descriptor. The probability-field head, soft-argmax decode, loss, confidence head, training data, epochs, and learning rate are all untouched, so attribution is purely capacity/depth.

**Gates verified before registration:** 972,993 params, ONNX 3.72 MiB (< 4 MiB), host latency proxy 2.91 ms (< 250 ms), forward/backward/loss smoke-tested. Training FLOPs rise ~7× (roughly 20–25 min/area on MPS).

**Prediction:** if capacity binds, worst-case median drops clearly below 2326 m (plausibly 1,000–2,000 m), bright buckets improving most; downside is bounded near the current best since a uniform field still decodes to the map center. A null would be equally valuable — it would rule out the last model-side family and point squarely at the training signal itself (sim-to-real relighting or lighting-conditional structure) for the next iterations.

`runs/pending_experiment.json` carries the full pre-registration including the gallery `eli5`, the six inference stages (only "Feature extractor" marked changed), and the architecture SVG in the shared visual language — red reserved for the encoder, skip arcs over the three residual stages, frozen endpoints in grey.

tool_input.command: cat runs/20260720_205622_iter1/metrics.json | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(json.dumps(d, indent=1)[:3000])"
tool_input.description: Read last kept run's metric breakdown

tool_input.command:
.venv/bin/python - <<'EOF'
import time, numpy as np, torch
from model.model import build_model, export_onnx, loss_fn

m = build_model()
n_params = sum(p.numel() for p in m.parameters())
print("params:", n_params)

# forward + loss smoke test
x = torch.rand(4, 3, 128, 128)
out, logits = m(x, return_logits=True)
tgt = torch.rand(4, 2)
l = loss_fn(out, logits, tgt)
l.backward()
print("forward/loss ok:", out.shape, logits.shape, float(l))

export_onnx(m, "/tmp/exp8_check.onnx")
import os
size = os.path.getsize("/tmp/exp8_check.onnx")
print(f"onnx bytes: {size} ({size/1024/1024:.2f} MiB, gate 4.00 MiB) -> {'PASS' if size <= 4*1024*1024 else 'FAIL'}")

import onnxruntime as ort
opts = ort.SessionOptions(); opts.intra_op_num_threads = 1; opts.inter_op_num_threads = 1
sess = ort.InferenceSession("/tmp/exp8_check.onnx", sess_options=opts, providers=["CPUExecutionProvider"])
xi = np.zeros((1, 3, 128, 128), np.float32)
for _ in range(3): sess.run(None, {"frame": xi})
ts = []
for _ in range(20):
    t0 = time.perf_counter(); sess.run(None, {"frame": xi}); ts.append((time.perf_counter()-t0)*1000)
lat = float(np.median(ts))
print(f"latency proxy: {lat:.2f} ms (gate 250 ms) -> {'PASS' if lat <= 250 else 'FAIL'}")
EOF
tool_input.description: Smoke-test new encoder: params, ONNX size gate, latency gate
Extract SVG and thumbnail it with qlmanage
Render SVG thumbnail to PNG for visual check
Render SVG thumbnail to PNG for visual check
